explanation: Gate acts as a central hub receiving requests from all external clients (Deck, Custom Script/API Caller, Halyard Daemon) and dispatching to nearly every downstream service (Keel, Orca, Kayenta, Rosco, Clouddriver, Front50, Fiat), making it a clear bottleneck with too many dependencies — a classic API Gateway overload. Orca similarly aggregates calls to multiple services (Echo, Clouddriver, Rosco, Fiat, Front50), acting as an orchestration monolith rather than a focused microservice. Fiat receives connections from a wide range of services (Gate, Orca, Clouddriver, Front50, Keel), suggesting it has become a cross-cutting dependency that many services rely on, which can indicate it has accumulated too many authorization/responsibility concerns. These services violate the single responsibility principle by becoming central coordination points, creating scalability and maintainability bottlenecks characteristic of the Microservice Greedy smell.
